claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'd like to inquire about. My eldest is Alex, who's 13, and my other child is Blake, who's 15.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children - Alex (13) and Blake (15).

Now, let me ask you: **Are you responsible for Alex and Blake? By this I mean, do they normally live with you, or do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent cost) towards looking after them each week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex lives with me at home. But Blake doesn't live with me - though I do help out financially with their upkeep each week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Alex lives with you, and you help financially with Blake's upkeep even though Blake doesn't live with you.

To understand Blake's situation better: **How much do you contribute towards Blake's upkeep each week? And do you know if anyone else (such as the person Blake lives with) is claiming or planning to claim Child Benefit for Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute about £26 a week towards Blake's upkeep - I help with clothes, food, things like that. As far as I know, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to understand a bit more about your living situation and status: **Do you normally live in the UK? And are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or any other immigration status I should know about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. 

Now, regarding Alex and Blake's education and training status: **Are either Alex or Blake currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? For example, are they in school, college (studying A-levels or similar), or doing an approved training course?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is still in school - just doing normal secondary school at 13. Blake is 15, so also still in school age.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Both children are still in secondary school, which is normal for their ages.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Have either Alex or Blake started living with a partner as a couple, or are either of them responsible for a child of their own?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. They're just normal kids - no partners, no children of their own.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final important question: **Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £60,000 per year? This is important because of the High Income Child Benefit Charge.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns that much.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake**.

### For Alex:
✓ Alex is 13 years old (under 16), in full-time secondary school, lives with you, and you're responsible for them. You meet all the eligibility requirements.

### For Blake:
✓ Blake is 15 years old (under 16) and in full-time secondary school. Even though Blake doesn't live with you, you contribute approximately £26 per week towards their upkeep, which meets the financial contribution threshold (the Child Benefit rate is around £25 per week for one child). Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, you are eligible to claim.

### Next Steps:
You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. Since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, you won't need to worry about the High Income Child Benefit Charge.

**Important note:** If someone else (such as the person Blake lives with) decides they want to claim Child Benefit for Blake, there are priority rules that determine who should receive it - typically the person the child lives with has priority. But based on your current situation, you are eligible to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), in full-time secondary school education, lives with you (the claimant), and has not started living with a partner or has a child of their own. You are a UK citizen living in the UK (Manchester). You meet the responsibility test as Alex normally lives with you. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 15 years old (under 16), in full-time secondary school education, has not started living with a partner or has a child of their own, and you are a UK citizen living in the UK. Although Blake does not live with you, you contribute approximately £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep, which meets the financial contribution threshold (Child Benefit rate for one child is currently around £25 per week). You meet the responsibility test through financial contribution at least equal to the Child Benefit amount. As far as you know, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, which means only one person would be claiming (priority rules apply if someone else also claims, but that's not the case here). Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with two children: Alex (13) and Blake (15). Both children are under 16, in full-time education, and have not started living with partners or have children of their own. Alex lives with the claimant, clearly meeting the responsibility test. Blake does not live with the claimant, but the claimant contributes approximately £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep, which meets the threshold of contributing at least the equivalent of Child Benefit. No one else is claiming for Blake. Neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, so the High Income Child Benefit Charge does not apply. The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for both children.

Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
